rulings.Case Title: ELLEN PAO VS. KLEINER PERKINS CAUFIELD & BYERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Precluding The Depositions Of Christina Lee And Aileen Lee And For An Order Quashing The Subpoena To Amanda Duckworth
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 2, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT KLEINER PERKINS CAUFIELD & BYERS LLC's Motion For A Protective Order Precluding The Depositions Of Christina Lee And Aileen Lee And For An Order Quashing The Subpoena To Amanda Duckworth. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. The Motion to Compel the deposition of Amanda Duckworth is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff may take the deposition of Duckworth. The topics of the deposition are limited as follows. Plaintiff may not ask questions regarding communications between Duckworth and Defense counsel. Plaintiff may ask questions regarding Duckworth's communications with third parties that refer or relate to plaintiff's employment, termination, and "communications to the press that plaintiff's allegations were wholly false when KPCB knew they were true." (Lawson Decl., Ex. K, Resp. to SI #11). The Motion to Compel the deposition of Aileen Lee is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff may take the deposition of Aileen Lee. Plaintiff may ask questions regarding plaintiff's employment, termination, and communications with Stephen Hirschfeld. (Lawless Decl., par. 2). Plaintiff may not inquire into Aileen Lee's personnel information. The parties are ordered to meet and confer on the RFP contained in the Amanda Duckworth and Aileen Lee notices of deposition. The parties should be prepared to discuss the RFP at the hearing. The Motion to Compel the deposition of Christine Lee is denied, without prejudice. Plaintiff has not presented any evidence that Christine Lee has any information that is relevant to plaintiff's liability or damages allegations. (See Opp. 9:3-4; Exelrod Decl., par. 3). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELLEN PAO VS. KLEINER PERKINS CAUFIELD & BYERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 2, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ELLEN PAO's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Plaintiff's Motion to Quash is granted in part and denied in part. As to both subpoenas, the Court has balanced Plaintiff's right to privacy against Defendant's stated reasons for needing the discovery, and rules as follows: Category 1: denied as moot in light of the parties' stipulation. Category 2: granted as it is not directly relevant to the present action. Category 3: granted because the request is overbroad and not directly relevant. Category 4: granted as such complaints could involve almost anything and hence are no directly relevant. Category 5: denied in part. The subpoena may only seek document referring to the present Pao v. KBCP lawsuit. Category 6: denied in part. The subpoena may only seek documents referring to Pao's compensation. Category 7: granted. Category 8: denied as moot in light of the parties' stipulation. Category 9: granted as the request is too vague. The Court finds no privacy waiver by Plaintiff for not objecting to the prior subpoena of Reddit, and notes that privacy waivers are not lightly found. (See, e.g., Heda v. Sup. Ct. (1990) 225 Cal.App.3d 525, 530.) As to the subpoena of Pao's former employer, BEA, the motion is granted in its entirety. The Court finds as a general matter that the discovery sought is too remote in time. In addition, Category 1 is overbroad and not directly relevant to the present action. Categories 2 and 3 are not directly relevant, and categories 4, 5, and 6 are overbroad and not directly relevant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDRE BARBASHOV et al VS. CINDERELLA BAKERY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 2, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT CINDERELLA BAKERY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Defendant has not met its initial burden of production. Defendants rely on the Declaration of Dr. Richard Clark in support of its argument that Plaintiffs cannot establish causation. However, the Clark Declaration is inadmissible because it does not "disclose the matter relied on in in forming the opinion expressed." (Kelley v. Trunk (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 519, 524.) Thus, Defendants have not presented evidence establishing "a prima facie showing of the nonexistence of any triable issue of material fact." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PING CHING WONG et al VS. TSUNG TSIN ASSOCIATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 2, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANTs EUGENE CHAN, BIANNE CHAN, NYUGET BACH DU, STEVEN YANG, EVA CHUK, LY K TAC, DAVID NGHIEM, THO HOA TRAN, NHUT SAP TSAN, LIN FU FUNG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication. The court denies summary judgment and grants and denies summary adjudication in part. The court grants summary adjudication against plaintiffs Ping Ching Wong and Michael Hou on the assault cause of action. Only plaintiff Ms. Mu Ling Lai can maintain this cause of action against defendant Ly K.Tac. They are the only tortfeasor and victim on the assault claim. Therefore, the court grants summary adjudication in favor of all of the individual defendants save Ly K. Tac on the assault claim. The court grants summary adjudication on the slander cause of action in favor of all of the individual defendants save defendant Eugene Chan. He is the only defendant that uttered the allegedly slanderous remark. The court rejects plaintiffs' reference to the corporate minutes as that theory of recovery is not alleged in the complaint. The court rejects defendants' privilege argument because that issue was not raised in the moving papers and only surfaced in the reply brief. The court grants summary adjudication in favor of all of the individual defendants on the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Plaintiffs fail to highlight extreme and outrageous conduct. Plaintiffs' remedy is to pursue its other causes of action. The court further notes that plaintiffs failed to provide an opposing separate statement in compliance with CCP 437c(b)(3) and that omission is also a basis for this ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PING CHING WONG et al VS. TSUNG TSIN ASSOCIATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Joinder)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 2, 2015, Line 6, INTERVENOR FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Joinder). The court denies summary judgment and grants and denies summary adjudication in part. The court denies summary adjudication on the assault and slander causes of action. Farmers Insurance Exchange, who intervened on behalf of the Tsung Tsin Association, Inc., fails to show that the Association is not responsible for Mr. Tac's and Mr. Chan's conduct. The court grants summary adjudication in favor of the Association on the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Plaintiffs fail to highlight extreme and outrageous conduct. Plaintiffs' remedy is to pursue its other causes of action. The court further notes that plaintiffs failed to provide an opposing separate statement in compliance with CCP 437c(b)(3) and that omission is also a basis for this ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
